channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] yifanfeng97/Hyper-Extract — Transform unstructured text into structured knowledge with LLMs. Graphs, hypergraphs, and spatio-temporal extractions — with one command.
item.description: Hyper-Extract uses LLMs to transform unstructured text into structured graphs, hypergraphs, and spatio-temporal data with a single command.

item.title: [Anthropic] Evaluating Claude’s bioinformatics research capabilities with BioMysteryBench
item.description: Evaluating Claude’s bioinformatics research capabilities with BioMysteryBench

item.title: [Latent Space] The Professor of Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP
item.description: The Professor of Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP

item.title: [OpenAI] New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
item.description: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises

item.title: [Hacker News] The founder of Craigslist has given away half a billion dollars
item.description: The founder of Craigslist has given away half a billion dollars

item.title: [Anthropic] Project Fetch: Phase two
item.description: Project Fetch: Phase two

item.title: [Hacker News] Show HN: Gerrymandle - Daily puzzle game where you redraw electoral districts
item.description: Show HN: Gerrymandle - Daily puzzle game where you redraw electoral districts

item.title: [Hacker News] Emacs 31 Is Around the Corner: The Changes I'm Daily Driving
item.description: Emacs 31 Is Around the Corner: The Changes I'm Daily Driving

item.title: [GitHub Trending] zai-org/GLM-5 — GLM-5: From Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering
item.description: GLM-5 transitions development from vibe coding to structured agentic engineering.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/withastro — The sandbox agent framework.
item.description: Astro's sandbox agent framework enables isolated testing and development of autonomous agents.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Kilo-Org/kilocode — Kilo is the all-in-one agentic engineering platform. Build, ship, and iterate faster with the most popular open source coding agent.
item.description: Kilo is an open-source agentic engineering platform for faster code development and deployment.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Kong/insomnia — The open-source, cross-platform API client for GraphQL, REST, WebSockets, SSE and gRPC. With Cloud, Local and Git storage.
item.description: Insomnia is an open-source API client supporting GraphQL, REST, WebSockets, SSE, and gRPC with cloud, local, and Git storage options.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] owainlewis/awesome-artificial-intelligence — A curated list of Artificial Intelligence (AI) courses, books, video lectures and papers.
item.description: Curated GitHub collection of AI resources: courses, books, video lectures, and academic papers.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Lightricks/LTX-2 — Official Python inference and LoRA trainer package for the LTX-2 audio–video generative model.
item.description: Lightricks released an official Python package for LTX-2, an audio-video generative model, supporting inference and LoRA training.

item.title: [OpenAI] Improving health intelligence in ChatGPT
item.description: Improving health intelligence in ChatGPT

item.title: [OpenAI] Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
item.description: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children

item.title: [Hacker News] The Australian Government to Require SMS/MMS Sender ID Registraion
item.description: The Australian Government to Require SMS/MMS Sender ID Registraion

item.title: [Hacker News] DeepSeek Introduces Vision
item.description: DeepSeek Introduces Vision

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] Midjourney Medical: scan your organs like you step on a scale
item.description: [AINews] Midjourney Medical: scan your organs like you step on a scale

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Gaussian Mixture Attention: Linear-Time Sequence Mixing via Probabilistic Latent Routing
item.description: Gaussian Mixture Attention reduces Transformer attention from O(N²) to O(NK) through probabilistic routing via learned mixture components.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Breaking the Solver Bottleneck: Training Task Generators at the Learnable Frontier
item.description: PROPEL trains task generators using a proxy model to predict learnability, replacing expensive solver rollouts and increasing frontier-difficulty tasks for code and software engineering.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CODEBLOCK: Learning to Supervise Code at the Right Granularity
item.description: CodeBlock selectively applies training loss to syntactically complete code blocks rather than individual tokens, achieving better performance while using only 1.9% of response tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Artemis: Anatomy-Resolved inTervention for Eliminating Multimodal NeuroImage confounderS
item.description: Artemis is a causal intervention framework that corrects for demographic confounders in multimodal neuroimaging data at the region level for graph neural networks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Link between Shock-wave Theory and Symmetry-reduced Stochastic Gradient Descent for Artificial Neural Networks
item.description: Mathematical framework connects shock-wave theory to neural network training dynamics via symmetry reduction and Hamilton-Jacobi equations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Attribution-Guided and Coverage-Maximized Pruning for Structural MoE Compression
item.description: Attribution-guided structural pruning framework compresses MoE models by identifying redundant channels within experts, achieving 5.27x memory reduction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Fisher Width: A Geometric Measure of Complexity on Statistical Manifolds
item.description: Researchers introduce Fisher width, a geometric complexity measure for statistical manifolds based on the Fisher information metric, extending Gaussian width theory.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DRIFT: Refining Instruction Data via On-Policy Data Attribution
item.description: DRIFT identifies high-value training data by applying on-policy influence functions to refine instruction data for LLMs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TRIDENT: Breaking the Hybrid-Safety-Physics Coupling for Provably Safe Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: TRIDENT presents a safe multi-agent reinforcement learning framework for cyber-physical systems with hybrid actions and physics dynamics, reducing violations by 95.5%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SAGE: Retain-Aware Post-Hoc Sanitization of Final Unlearning Vector
item.description: SAGE provides post-hoc correction for LLM unlearning updates, improving retention performance without rerunning original unlearning methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Ghost Attractor Networks: Basin-Structured Dynamical Decoders for Closed-Loop Sequential Generation
item.description: Ghost Attractor Networks are dynamical decoders that match billion-parameter Diffusion Transformers' offline accuracy for robotic control generation using 462x fewer parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Survey on Data-Driven Models for Soil Moisture Regression and Classification
item.description: Survey of AI-based models for soil moisture estimation, covering statistical, geostatistical, machine learning, deep learning, and Bayesian approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Enhanced Graph Neural Networks using K-Hop Gaussian Diffusion
item.description: K-Hop Gaussian diffusion kernel balances local-global information propagation to improve GNNs on noisy and complex graphs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ASTRA: A Scalable Next-Generation ATCO Training Simulator with Autonomous Simpilots
item.description: ASTRA is an automated air traffic control training simulator using locally-adapted speech models and AI performance evaluation for Singaporean-accented aviation communications.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SAE Interventions are Unreliable: Post-Intervention Recovery of Suppressed Behavior
item.description: Sparse autoencoder interventions cannot reliably prevent model misbehavior because models recover suppressed behaviors through alternative pathways in activation space.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Why SWAVE May Not Be All You Need:A Concept-Evolution Retrospective on Complex-Valued Recurrent Language Models
item.description: SWave, a complex-valued RNN language model, requires architectural changes as key components proved unnecessary and design assumptions created failure modes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Neural Network Implementation of the Renormalization Group for Fault Diagnosis with Class Imbalance
item.description: RGNet, a neural network based on renormalization group theory, performs hierarchical feature compression for fault prediction with imbalanced data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Self-CTRL: Self-Consistency Training with Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Self-CTRL optimizes language model consistency by aligning self-explanations with behavior via reinforcement learning, improving auditability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ThousandWorlds: A benchmark for climate emulation of potentially habitable exoplanets
item.description: ThousandWorlds benchmark provides 1800 climate model simulations for training machine learning emulators to predict potentially habitable exoplanet atmospheric properties.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Do Time Series Foundation Model Benchmarks Hide Regime-Dependent Failures? Evidence from Traffic Speed Forecasting
item.description: Time series foundation models fail during traffic transitions but aggregate benchmarks mask these failures; regime-stratified evaluation and bimodal mixture augmentation reveal and address them.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Sparse Features to Trustworthy Proxies: Certifying SAE-Based Interpretability
item.description: Researchers develop a certification framework for sparse autoencoders that verifies when extracted interpretable features faithfully represent language model behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SCOPE-FL: A Strategy-proof Chain-based Optimal pareto efficient Federated Learning System
item.description: SCOPE-FL applies game theory and blockchain to hierarchical federated learning, achieving Pareto-efficient client selection and strategy-proof reward distribution.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LLMZero: Discovering Adaptive Training Strategies for RL Post-Training via LLM Agents
item.description: LLMZero discovers RL post-training strategies via LLM agents performing tree search, achieving 9-140% improvements over base models on tested tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MOLAR: Learning Multimodal Molecular Representations from Noisy Labels
item.description: MOLAR learns multimodal molecular representations robust to noisy labels by inferring clean properties separately from recorded observations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] P$^2$CE: Model-Agnostic Plausible Pareto-Optimal Counterfactual Explanations
item.description: P²CE generates model-agnostic Pareto-optimal counterfactual explanations balancing feasibility, plausibility, and computational efficiency.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Measurement noise limits the advantage of nonlinear models over linear models in biomedical prediction
item.description: Measurement noise in biomedical data erases nonlinear structure faster than linear structure, negating flexible models' advantage over simple linear regression.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Signature filtering: a lightweight enhancement for statistical watermark detection in large language models
item.description: Signature filtering improves watermark detection in large language models by identifying and removing tokens that weaken watermark signals.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Beyond Prediction: Tail-Aware Scheduling for LLM Inference
item.description: Researchers introduce a distribution-aware, prediction-free LLM scheduler that reduces P99 tail latency by up to 50% using statistical signals instead of length predictions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TMR-GGNN: Credit Card Fraud Detection based on Time-Aware Multi-Relational Guided Graph Neural Network
item.description: Graph neural network TMR-GGNN detects credit card fraud by modeling temporal customer-merchant-device relationships using contrastive learning on imbalanced data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Cross-Model VLM-Judge Protocol for Single-Image 3D Mesh Quality (and Why Cheap Proxies Fall Short)
item.description: Researchers validate a VLM-judge protocol for evaluating 3D mesh quality and find common cheap proxies ineffective.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Continuous Audio Thinking for Large Audio Language Models
item.description: Researchers propose Continuous Audio Thinking framework enabling large audio language models to preserve acoustic information without additional computational cost.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Redact or Keep? A Fully Local AI Cascade for Educational Dialogue De-Identification
item.description: Fully local cascade framework for educational dialogue de-identification achieves 0.958 F1, exceeding LLM baselines while maintaining privacy on laptops.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SproutRAG: Attention-Guided Tree Search with Progressive Embeddings for Long-Document RAG
item.description: SproutRAG improves RAG efficiency by using learned attention to organize documents into hierarchical semantic units, enabling multi-granularity retrieval without additional LLM calls.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Want Better Synthetic Data? Steer It: Activation Steering for Low-Resource Language Generation
item.description: Researchers use activation steering on language models to generate more diverse and higher-quality synthetic data for low-resource languages without costly few-shot prompting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] JetFlow: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting
item.description: JetFlow breaks speculative decoding's scaling ceiling through branch-wise causal conditioning, achieving 9.64x speedup on dense and MoE models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CoreMem: Riemannian Retrieval and Fisher-Guided Distillation for Long-Term Memory in Dialogue Agents
item.description: CoreMem combines Riemannian retrieval and Fisher-guided distillation to enable efficient long-term memory for dialogue agents on edge devices with improved reasoning performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] VISUALSKILL: Multimodal Skills for Computer-Use Agents
item.description: VISUALSKILL hierarchical skill library combining visual figures and text improves computer-use agent performance 15.3 points over baseline.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LLM Parameters for Math Across Languages: Shared or Separate?
item.description: Multilingual LLMs use partially shared mathematical reasoning parameters across languages, with strongest overlap in intermediate layers and systematic language-dependent variation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Montreal Forced Aligner and the state of speech-to-text alignment in 2026
item.description: Montreal Forced Aligner 3.0 achieves state-of-the-art forced alignment performance with expanded language coverage and <15ms boundary errors across benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Possible or Definite? A Benchmark for Evaluating Diagnostic Uncertainty Preservation in Clinical Text
item.description: Large language models fail to preserve diagnostic uncertainty in clinical text, maintaining accuracy of uncertainty expressions less than 50% of the time.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PreUnlearn: Auditing Collateral Knowledge Damage Before Large Language Model Unlearning
item.description: Study shows LLM unlearning damages related knowledge across domains; proposes pre-unlearning auditing to identify and assess collateral damage before execution.
